expectations.text: Defines who the AI is with role, domain, tone, and audience
expectations.evidence: Core Identity: knowledgeable and friendly customer support agent, professional/empathetic/concise tone

expectations.text: Describes the environment, tools, and knowledge sources available
expectations.evidence: Mentions knowledge base but doesn't describe tools, escalation paths, or product scope in a dedicated context section

expectations.text: Contains numbered hard rules including feature fabrication and refund prohibitions
expectations.evidence: 5 NEVER rules including rule 1 (fabricate features) and rule 2 (refunds)

expectations.text: Specifies how the AI should format its responses
The refund constraint covers paraphrases like credit, compensation, making it right
expectations.evidence: Refund rule says 'cannot issue refunds, credit payments, or promise any monetary compensation' but doesn't cover paraphrases like 'making it right' or 'take care of the charge'

expectations.text: Handles edge cases like social engineering or ambiguous refund requests
expectations.evidence: Tricky Scenarios table covers: unknown feature, refund request, feature timeline, competitor comparison, refund threat, angry user

expectations.text: Provides tone mapping or behavioral guidelines for different customer emotions
expectations.evidence: No tone mapping table. Only general tone guidance (professional, empathetic, patient)

expectations.text: Explicitly names prompting techniques used (Definition Lock, Delimiter Pattern, etc.)
expectations.evidence: No named techniques. Uses general principles (Positive Framing, Concrete Scenarios, Self-Check, Escalation Paths, Layered Constraints)
